title: Basic Photo Editing
drDigitalIntro: You don't need to be a professional to improve your photos! Basic edits can make a big difference. Rotate: turns the photo 90 degrees if it's sideways. Crop: trims the edges to focus on the main subject. Brightness: makes the photo lighter or darker. Filters: applies a color effect. The best part: you can always revert to the original if you don't like the result!

playgroundTask.goal: You edited a photo and reverted it

steps.say: Click the Mountain Hike photo to open it.
Click Rotate to turn the photo 90 degrees.
Click Crop to trim the photo.
Adjust the brightness by clicking Brightness.
Apply a filter to change the mood of the photo.
Click Revert to undo all changes and restore the original.

drDigitalSuccess: You edited a photo and reverted it! The original is always preserved — you can always undo edits.
drDigitalHint: Open a photo first, then use the editing buttons that appear. Use Revert to undo all changes.
